name: Scales v. United States
question: Does a Communist Party member's conviction under the Smith Act, which makes a felony the knowing membership in organizations advocating the violent or forceful overthrow of the United States government, violate the Fifth Amendment's due process clause in light of the apparent protections afforded to such members under the Internal Security Act?
description: Scales was prosecuted and convicted of a felony for his membership in the Communist Party, which advocated the violent overthrow of the government, and was illegal under the Smith Act.
